HelpHub.overview: HelpHub is a modern B2B SaaS platform focused on AI-assisted customer support workflows for mid-market SaaS and fintech. Core modules include ticket routing, knowledge base automation, and in-app chat.
product_modules: Self-serve knowledge base with semantic search
Integrations: Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Jira, Zendesk data bridge

go_to_market.pricing: Tiered per-agent with platform fee; usage-based add-ons for AI features
go_to_market.sales_model: Hybrid PLG with SDR/AE assist for accounts >100 seats
go_to_market.churn_commentary: Logo churn low; revenue churn offset by expansion via AI add-ons

financial_notes.arr_commentary: ARR grew from $8.9M to $12.0M in the last 12 months
financial_notes.gross_margin_drivers: High-margin AI features; modest third-party LLM costs with volume discounts
financial_notes.working_capital: Positive cash conversion; prepaid annual contracts dominant

risk_assessment.customer_concentration: Top 10 contribute 38% of ARR; largest single customer at 14%
risk_assessment.technology: Modern cloud-native stack; strong internal MLOps; no deprecated components noted
risk_assessment.compliance: SOC 2 Type II in progress, expected completion within 6 months

integration_risks: Potential overlap with buyer’s existing analytics module
Salesforce integration mapping differences may require data model reconciliation
Need to align AI model governance and prompt libraries post-close

team_notes.founder_status: Founder-CEO willing to transition for 9 months and then advisory
team_notes.key_leaders: VP Engineering with strong ML background; Head of CS with low attrition

assumptions: Retention remains >100% NRR with AI add-on attach rate sustaining
No near-term need for on-prem deployments
Buyer can cross-sell into existing enterprise accounts

TicketFlow.overview: TicketFlow provides legacy ticketing and knowledge base software primarily for traditional SaaS and manufacturing support teams. The product is stable but behind modern UI/UX expectations.
product_modules: Legacy on-prem option for a subset of customers
customer_segments: SMB to lower mid-market support teams
Manufacturing and field service orgs with legacy IT policies

go_to_market.pricing: Seat-based with discounts for on-prem installs
go_to_market.sales_model: Channel-heavy with a few regional resellers
go_to_market.churn_commentary: Stable logos but weak upsell; some pressure from modern SaaS competitors

financial_notes.arr_commentary: ARR at $7.5M with moderate growth from renewal pricing actions
financial_notes.gross_margin_drivers: Lower margins due to on-prem support and older infra
financial_notes.capex_needs: UI rewrite and cloud migration would require notable investment

risk_assessment.customer_concentration: Top 10 at 52% of ARR; top customer contributes 28%
risk_assessment.key_contracts: Largest customer’s 3-year agreement expires in 8 months; renewal risk flagged
risk_assessment.technology: Monolithic Ruby 2.5 backend, AngularJS 1.x frontend; significant tech debt
risk_assessment.compliance: Ad-hoc security practices; no SOC 2; basic vendor questionnaires only

integration_risks: Migration from monolith to service-oriented architecture will be complex
UI rewrite required to meet modern standards
Data model mismatches with buyer’s CRM and billing systems
On-prem customers complicate support model post-close

team_notes.founder_status: Founder unwilling to remain post-close (0 months)
team_notes.turnover: Elevated attrition in engineering and support over the last year
team_notes.key_person_risk: Two senior engineers hold critical system knowledge

assumptions: Earnout mechanics would be contentious given tech rewrite timeline
Retention risk if largest customer churns at renewal
Potential upside from channel refresh if product modernized

SupportNexus.overview: SupportNexus focuses on workflow automation and agent assistance for vertical SaaS in healthcare and govtech. Strong compliance posture but facing recent growth headwinds.
customer_segments: Govtech platforms with strict compliance needs

go_to_market.pricing: Hybrid per-seat and per-workflow pricing; enterprise minimums
go_to_market.sales_model: Direct enterprise with long sales cycles

financial_notes.arr_commentary: ARR $4.2M; YoY decline driven by loss of two logos after budget cuts
financial_notes.gross_margin_drivers: Efficient cloud spend; moderate support costs
financial_notes.pipeline: Improving pipeline but elongated decision cycles

risk_assessment.revenue_trend: Negative growth of -12% YoY; stabilization plan underway
risk_assessment.customer_concentration: Top 10 at 41% of ARR; top customer at 19%
risk_assessment.technology: Modern Python FastAPI and Vue 3; some legacy cron jobs scheduled for refactor

integration_risks: Custom compliance workflows may slow standardization
Overlap with buyer’s automation tool could cause positioning confusion
Data residency requirements for certain customers

team_notes.founder_status: Founder willing to transition for 6 months and facilitate key relationships
team_notes.turnover: Within normal range; retention programs in place

assumptions: Stabilization achievable if top customers retained
Cross-sell into buyer’s healthcare portfolio can restore growth
Refactor of legacy cron jobs can be completed within 90 days post-close
